name: Apple (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw apple, with skin). concentration_mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL (water-rich matrix, density ~1).

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 171688 (SR Legacy, Apples, raw, with skin)
components.evidence: matrix / solvent, ~86% w/w

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon source; corroborated by Serbia cultivar HPLC (3.5-5.6 g/100g, doi:10.3390/foods13152309/PMC11276096)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: HPLC-corroborated 1.5-2.4 g/100g (PMC11276096)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: HPLC-corroborated 1.6-3.3 g/100g (PMC11276096)

components.name: D-sorbitol
components.source: Serbia cultivar HPLC, PMC11276096 (2.3-3.6 g/kg FW, midpoint 0.30 g/100g)
components.evidence: apple-characteristic polyol, fermentable; absent from USDA panel and from the FooDB profile

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: reported as 0 in raw apple; FooDB 'Maltose' entry not a real free sugar here

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: free galactose ~0 (present only as galacturonan/pectin backbone)

components.name: starch
components.evidence: near-zero in ripe apple (converted to sugars during ripening)

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Aprea/PMC6316603 (cultivated Malus mean 5.26 mg/g FW) + Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (2.7-7.0 g/kg)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid, up to ~90% of apple acidity; major secondary carbon/energy source. Missing from FooDB profile

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (76-527 mg/kg FW, midpoint ~300 mg/kg)
components.evidence: second-most-abundant free acid; also the biosynthetic precursor of chlorogenic acid. Missing from FooDB profile

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (47-132 mg/kg FW); PMC6316603 notes near-undetectable in cultivated apple
components.evidence: minor in Malus domestica (unlike citrus); included for completeness

components.name: shikimic acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (69-412 mg/kg FW, midpoint ~240 mg/kg)
components.evidence: minor free acid, shikimate-pathway intermediate

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (119-194 mg/kg FW, midpoint 156 mg/kg)
components.evidence: minor TCA-cycle acid

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (32-305 mg/kg FW, midpoint ~168 mg/kg)
components.evidence: minor TCA-cycle acid

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Serbia cultivars PMC11276096 (14-44 mg/kg FW, midpoint 29 mg/kg)
components.evidence: trace organic acid

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: also functions as an organic acid; listed once here

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: very low-protein matrix

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: DOMINANT amino acid in apple (aspartate/asparagine pool)

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: principal mineral

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: reported ~0

components.name: fluoride
components.evidence: trace

components.name: vitamin C (see L-ascorbate above)
components.evidence: cross-referenced to organic-acid entry

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: absent (plant food); flag if a model requires B12 supplementation

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: present per FooDB; USDA does not quantify
components.evidence: trace, unquantified

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence: genuine but unquantified in USDA; minor

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: apple is a very low-fat matrix

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: dominant fatty acid

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: includes ~1 g pectin (galacturonan) - partly fermentable by pectinolytic microbes; see keep_uncovered 'pectin'

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant metal, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: animal sterol, biologically implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Cardiolipin
remove.reason: mitochondrial/bacterial membrane lipid, not a dietary nutrient of apple; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Estrone cytosol
remove.reason: animal steroid hormone, implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Phosphatidylcholine
remove.reason: membrane lipid listed without measurable amount; not a growth-relevant nutrient at apple lipid levels (0.17 g/100g total fat); FooDB predicted

remove.name: Phosphatidylglycerol (uncovered)
remove.reason: membrane lipid, FooDB predicted, not a dietary nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol (uncovered)
remove.reason: synthetic contraceptive drug, impossible endogenous apple constituent; database artifact

remove.name: Silicon (uncovered)
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element, not a microbial growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+ / Cobalt
remove.reason: ultratrace, non-nutritive at apple levels (no B12 in apple); FooDB noise

remove.name: Methylamine / Ethanamine / Diethylamine
remove.reason: trace volatile amines, not growth-relevant nutrients

missing_added: malic acid (0.50 g/100g - dominant organic acid, was absent)
D-sorbitol (0.30 g/100g - apple-characteristic polyol, was absent)
quantified individual sugars: D-fructose 5.90, D-glucose 2.43, sucrose 2.07 g/100g
keep_uncovered: chlorogenic acid / 5-caffeoylquinic acid / cryptochlorogenic acid (major apple polyphenol)
caffeic acid, p-coumaroylquinic acids, o-coumaric acid, ferulic acid, syringic acid, gallic acid
quercetin and glycosides (quercetin 3-xyloside, avicularin, cyanidin, cyanidin 3-galactoside)
procyanidins B1/B2/B5/B7/C1 and trimer EEC (condensed tannins)
pectin / pectic acid (galacturonan - partly fermentable polysaccharide, no single BiGG met)
amygdalin (cyanogenic glycoside, seeds; genuine but flesh-negligible)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171688 (SR Legacy, Apples, raw, with skin) - full proximate/sugar/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
Aprea et al., organic-acid survey of cultivated vs wild Malus, PMC6316603 (malic ~5.26 mg/g FW, citric near-undetectable)

Sugar & organic-acid + polyphenol chemometric study of conventional apple cultivars (Serbia), PMC11276096 (malic 2.7-7.0 g/kg, quinic 76-527, citric 47-132, shikimic 69-412, succinic 119-194, fumaric 32-305, oxalic 14-44 mg/kg; sorbitol 2.3-3.6 g/kg)
FooDB FOOD00105 (source of the original component list)
notes: Dominant carbon sources are the free sugars fructose (5.9 g/100g) > glucose (2.4) > sucrose (2.1), plus the apple-characteristic polyol sorbitol (~0.30). The dominant organic acid (and major secondary energy source) is malic acid (~0.5 g/100g, ~90% of acidity); citric is minor in Malus domestica unlike citrus. The original FooDB profile was missing ALL organic acids and sorbitol and carried heavy-metal, animal-sterol, and synthetic-drug artifacts (removed). Apple is a low-protein (0.26 g), low-fat (0.17 g), K/malate-dominated acidic fruit medium; a metabolic model may need vitamin B12 supplemented (absent).
